title: Semantic MCP surface — helix.* tools over the snapshot
description:
Expose a semantic MCP surface (not raw bd passthrough) so any agent — Cursor, Aider, Zed, future — can consume beads-helix without learning the bd CLI.

## Proposed surface
- helix.get_snapshot(projectId)
- helix.get_prime_bundle(issueId)
- helix.evaluate_refinement(issueId)
- helix.propose_close_checkpoint(issueId)
- helix.search_related(query, scope)
- helix.render_bd_commands(patch)  # advisory — returns the bd commands, doesn't execute

## Why this matters
- A thin MCP wrapper around bd show/list/update creates tool-chatter and leaks CLI instability into agents.
- Semantic tools are opinionated and require versioning, but compress 8-10 low-level calls into one stable operation.
- This is the exposure step that turns beads-helix into something other agent tools actually consume. The 'TCP/IP of agent work' framing only becomes real once this ships.

## DNA strand
Dual-format visibility — the MCP surface is the agent's format; the deck UI is the human's format. Same snapshot, two renderers.

## Depends on
- PrimeBundle/CloseCheckpoint schemas (beads-helix-klt)
- Context compiler

## Source
docs/research/brainstorm-roadmap-2026-04-20.md §Phase B

title: Deterministic context compiler for PrimeBundle selection
description:
PrimeBundle selection should be rules-based and budget-aware, not a freeform prompt. Select: current issue, open dependents, recently closed dependencies, relevant memories, git diff, recent close checkpoints, unresolved questions. Optionally run an LLM as a compression pass over already-selected material — never as the selector.

## Why this matters
- Deterministic selection is auditable; LLM-only selection drifts and is hard to debug.
- Codex (brainstorm 2026-04-20): 'the right split is deterministic selection, model summarization'.
- This is the mechanism that turns the snapshot from a read-model into a compiler output consumable by any agent.

## DNA strand
Convention over infrastructure — ship an opinionated selection policy, not a configurable ML pipeline.

## Depends on
- beads-helix-klt (PrimeBundle schema)
- Session ledger (above)

## Source

title: Session ledger — append-only PrimeBundle/CloseCheckpoint events
description:
Extend beads-helix-klt: treat PrimeBundle and CloseCheckpoint as *event types*, not just schemas. Store them as append-only artifacts keyed by (issue_id, session_id, agent_id).

## Why this matters
- All three AI providers in the 2026-04-20 brainstorm converged on this: checkpoints are events that form a ledger. From the ledger we can derive 'what changed', 'what was learned', 'what needs routing', 'what context the next agent should inherit'.
- Codex specifically: Dolt's cell-level merge semantics mean same-key JSON edits still conflict. Append-only rows sidestep parallel-agent merge pain entirely.

## DNA strand
Dual-format visibility + Layer not fork. The ledger is the substrate; humans view it through the deck, Claude queries it via MCP.

## Out of scope (for now)
- Ledger reducers that roll events into authoritative issue mutations (later phase — needs proposal/review flow)
- UI replay mode

## Depends on
- beads-helix-klt (schemas must land first)

title: helix CLI silently no-ops when invoked via npm-link symlink
description:
## Summary

When beads-helix is installed via `npm link` (or `npm install -g`), the `helix` binary on PATH is a symlink to `dist/cli.js`. Running `helix <anything>` exits 0 with no output — `main()` never executes.

## Reproduction

```bash
cd /path/to/beads-helix
npm run build
npm link
# anywhere:
helix                    # expected: usage + exit 1
helix snapshot path      # expected: prints snapshot path
# actual: silent exit 0 in both cases

# Control — works when bypassing the symlink:
node /path/to/beads-helix/dist/cli.js snapshot path   # prints path correctly
```

## Root cause

`src/cli.ts:179` (compiled to `dist/cli.js:159`):

```ts
if (import.meta.url === pathToFileURL(process.argv[1] ?? '').href) {
  main(process.argv.slice(2)).catch(...)
}
```

- `import.meta.url` resolves to the **target** file: `file:///.../beads-helix/dist/cli.js`
- `process.argv[1]` is the **symlink** path: `/.../nvm/.../bin/helix`
- `pathToFileURL(argv1)` → `file:///.../bin/helix` (encoded path, symlink-relative)
- Equality check fails → `main()` never called → silent exit 0

## Proposed fix

Resolve the symlink before comparing:

```ts
import { realpathSync } from 'node:fs';

const entry = process.argv[1] ? pathToFileURL(realpathSync(process.argv[1])).href : '';
if (import.meta.url === entry) {
  main(process.argv.slice(2)).catch(...)
}
```

## Impact

Hard-blocks any user who installs via `npm link` or `npm install -g beads-helix`. The only working invocation is `node <absolute-path-to-dist/cli.js>`, which is not documented as the intended usage.

Found while dogfooding beads-helix as dev scaffolding in zed-review (learning-capture goal). First concrete friction in the pre-1.0 churn bucket flagged during Define.

## Environment

- node v22.22.2 (nvm via Herd)
- macOS Darwin 25.0.0
- beads-helix HEAD: 3e9e3ce6438e78e95f445f250d18115f0c6e97c1
- npm link from /Users/florianriquelme/Repos/mine/beads-helix

## Acceptance criteria

- [ ] `helix` (no args) prints usage to stderr and exits 1
- [ ] `helix snapshot path` prints path and exits 0 (when run via symlink)
- [ ] Test covers: invocation via symlink (realpath differs from argv[1])
close_reason: Fixed by realpathSync resolution on argv[1] before file-URL comparison. Verified: helix (no args) prints usage + exit 1, helix snapshot path prints path + exit 0 via npm-link symlink. zed-review dogfooding resumed.

title: /helix:start <id> — deterministic prime bundle
description:
A slash command that deterministically composes an issue-scoped prime bundle on session/issue start. Same inputs, same outputs, every time. No AI smartness — determinism IS the feature.

Pulls:
- bd show <id> (issue body, DNA strand, acceptance criteria)
- bd list --json filtered to closed deps (backward context from their CloseCheckpoint notes)
- bd list --json filtered to open dependents (forward purpose)
- bd memories <keyword> with keywords derived deterministically from issue title/DNA/filenames
- Relevant recent commits touching files mentioned in the issue

Emits a PrimeBundle JSON + compact markdown rendering for Claude.

User problem: bd memories works and is valuable, but Claude surfaces it organically and inconsistently across sessions. Determinism removes the coin-flip.

Depends on: PrimeBundle schema definition (see sibling issue). Depends on /helix:close shipping first so CloseCheckpoint notes exist to consume.

title: Define PrimeBundle + CloseCheckpoint schemas in src/shared/types/
description:
Two canonical JSON shapes that both /helix:close and /helix:start emit, and that the flight deck renders. Ship BEFORE implementing either command so we don't end up with drift between Claude's markdown view and the UI's rendered view.

Proposed shapes (from round-2 debate, Codex):

PrimeBundle (emitted by /helix:start):
{ schema_version: 1, issue, memories[], open_dependents[], closed_dependencies[], unresolved[] }

CloseCheckpoint (emitted by /helix:close):
{ schema_version: 1, changed, learned, next, routed_to[] }

Both versioned from day 1. Location: src/shared/types/ or src/shared/. Exported from the snapshot package.

title: Start using maturity labels in this repo
description: Bake the maturity pipeline (idea/refined/ready) into CLAUDE.md as enforced convention. Triage all existing open issues to their correct maturity level. Include a learning-mode clause so friction gets flagged and logged for future tooling decisions.
acceptance_criteria: (1) CLAUDE.md contains maturity pipeline rules (stages, gates, create/close enforcement). (2) All open issues have accurate maturity labels. (3) Learning-mode clause exists so friction is flagged and logged via bd remember.
close_reason: All 3 acceptance criteria met: CLAUDE.md has maturity pipeline rules with stage definitions and create/close enforcement, all 6 open issues triaged to accurate labels, learning-mode clause active for friction logging.

title: Research: bd label support for maturity pipeline
description: Investigate whether bd labels can carry the maturity pipeline (idea/refined/ready). Test bd create -l idea, bd list filtering by label, bd update to change labels. Determine if any beads-side work is needed.
acceptance_criteria: Know definitively: (1) can create issues with -l idea/refined/ready, (2) can filter bd list by label, (3) can update labels on existing issues. Document any gaps.
notes:
## Research Findings (2026-04-11)

### Test 1: Create issues with maturity labels ✅
- `bd create -l idea` works perfectly
- Label appears in `bd show` output under LABELS section
- Multiple labels can be set at creation time

### Test 2: Filter bd list by label ✅
- `bd list -l <label>` filters by label (AND mode — must have ALL specified labels)
- `bd list --label-any <l1> --label-any <l2>` for OR filtering
- `bd list --no-labels` finds unlabeled issues
- `bd list --label-pattern 'tech-*'` supports glob patterns
- `bd list --label-regex 'idea|refined'` supports regex patterns

### Test 3: Update labels on existing issues ✅
- `bd update <id> --add-label refined --remove-label idea` works in a single command
- `bd tag <id> <label>` is shorthand for adding a label
- `bd label remove <id> <label>` for removal
- `bd label propagate` can cascade labels from parent to children

### Additional capabilities discovered
- `bd label list-all` shows all labels with issue counts
- `bd label list <id>` shows labels for a specific issue
- `bd label add <label> <id1> <id2>...` bulk-adds to multiple issues

### Conclusion
**No beads-side work needed.** Labels fully support the maturity pipeline (idea → refined → ready). All CRUD operations and filtering work out of the box.
close_reason: All three acceptance criteria confirmed. Labels fully support maturity pipeline with no beads-side work needed.

title: SSE per-project channels + selective Board invalidation
description:
Observable tech debt surfaced during 2026-04-20 roadmap brainstorm. Not paradigm-level, but will block federation scale.

## The gaps
1. src/server/sse.ts broadcasts with Promise.all, no per-client queue or backpressure. One slow client delays everyone.
2. src/server/index.ts already computes changedIssueIds[], but web/src/components/Board.tsx still invalidates the full snapshot on every SSE tick.
3. No project partitioning — every connected client gets every project's events.

## Why this matters now
- Fine for single-project today. Broken the moment the Level 1 deck (beads-helix-wso) connects to multiple project sidecars.
- Federation (cross-repo initiative graph) multiplies the problem.

## Fix sketch
- Per-project SSE channels: /api/projects/:id/events
- Per-client write queue with drop-oldest on slow consumers
- Board subscribes to changedIssueIds[] and invalidates only affected query keys

## DNA strand
Drop-in adoption — the deck has to stay snappy as project count grows.

## Source
docs/research/brainstorm-roadmap-2026-04-20.md §Non-Obvious Constraints #2

title: Policy-as-data — replace close-gate hook with schema-backed evaluators
description:
Maturity gates, routing heuristics, and refinement scoring currently live in .claude/hooks/helix-close-gate.sh (shell) and .claude/commands/helix/refine.md (LLM prompt). Consolidate them as schema-backed rules + evaluators run by the sidecar.

## Why this matters
- Codex (brainstorm 2026-04-20): 'Use policy-as-data, not more hooks.' Shell scripts don't compose, don't version, and bypass easily (--force).
- Policies become testable, shareable (see DNA strands package ecosystem), and queryable from both the MCP surface and the UI.

## Scope
- Model: Policy { id, applies_to: PolicyScope, criteria: RuleSet, action: advise|block }
- Evaluator runs in sidecar; exposed via helix.evaluate_* MCP tools
- Hook shells become thin wrappers that call the evaluator

## DNA strand
Convention over infrastructure — policies are data shipped with the tool, not code to fork.

title: Agent lanes — structured RefinementProposal + CloseCheckpoint review flow
description:
Give each agent a session lane. Agents emit structured proposals (RefinementProposal, CloseCheckpoint) into the append-only ledger. Helix detects semantic conflicts before merge, clusters duplicate discoveries, shows divergent refinements, and asks humans to decide only at convergence points.

## Proposed RefinementProposal shape
{ problem_statement, dna_link, done_definition, open_questions, confidence, proposed_patch, provenance }

## Why this matters
- Direct agent mutation is fast but low-trust. Proposal + review is slower but preserves dual-format trust.
- Enables N agents working in parallel without a central scheduler — the ledger plus Dolt merge semantics are the coordination fabric.
- Codex (brainstorm 2026-04-20): 'paradigm shift from issue tracker to coordination fabric'.

## DNA strand
Dual-format visibility + Convention over infrastructure — proposals are schema-backed, not a bespoke workflow engine.

## Depends on
- Session ledger
- Semantic MCP surface (agents submit proposals via helix.propose_*)

title: Cross-repo federation — project_id-canonical identity + worktree discovery
description:
Augment the project registry with auto-discovery so the Level 1 deck can reason about a workspace, not just an explicit list.

## Discovery sources
- Explicit registry (today's ~/.helix/projects.json)
- git worktree list --porcelain on known roots
- Sibling-dir scan under a configured workspace root
- repos.additional / federation config if present

## Identity model
- Canonical: project_id
- Path is an observed location, not identity
- Distinguish registered / observed / active-now states

## Why this matters
- Same project in multiple worktrees, moved paths, symlinks, temp dirs — all normal. Using path as identity breaks immediately.
- Morning priming shifts from 'which project should I open' to 'which fronts are hot/blocked/ready across my workspace'.

## DNA strand
Project-agnostic + Drop-in adoption — discovery should just work, no per-project config required.

## Related
- Extends beads-helix-wso (L1 priming view)

## Non-obvious constraint
.beads/last-touched is not enough for cross-repo truth. git pull / dolt pull / out-of-band changes bypass watched-file invalidation. Needs periodic reconciliation.

title: Proper npm dev server: Vite HMR + live Hono API + SSE

title: Open board cards into an issue detail view
description:
## Problem statement

When I click an issue card on the board, I currently get a copied ID instead of the context I need to decide what to do next. I cannot inspect description, notes, design, or dependency state without breaking flow and switching tools. The board is good for scanning, weak for understanding *why* an issue matters, what blocks it, and what it affects. I want to understand an issue well enough to act — without losing my place on the board.

## DNA strand

**Dual-format visibility.** The detail view gives the developer a readable surface to internalize the "why" of an issue, while the routeable `/p/:pid/i/:iid` URL and `Copy bd update` command keep it usable from Claude sessions and the terminal. One feature, two formats.

## Scope

### In scope
- Canonical child route `/p/$pid/i/$iid`
- Card click opens detail (replaces current click=copy-id behavior)
- Short-ID becomes its own button with `stopPropagation()` + `c` keyboard shortcut
- Desktop: right drawer (Radix `Dialog` with `modal={false}`) over mounted board
- Narrow screen: full-page detail
- Close via Esc / outside click / X / browser back → `/p/$pid` with search params (priority, q) preserved
- `GET /api/issue/:id?projectId=…` sliced from the existing snapshot file (no new Dolt queries)
- Bump `_meta.schema_version`; upgrade `snapshot-changed` SSE payload to `{ projectId, generatedAt, changedIssueIds[] }` for selective invalidation
- Render title, description, notes, design, dependencies, dependents — read-only
- Markdown via `react-markdown` + `remark-gfm` + `rehype-sanitize`
- `design` field inside collapsed `<details>` with first-line preview
- `Copy bd update <id> --notes/--description …` button as terminal bridge
- **Dependency Weather** block with three rails: open blockers · closed deps with inline notes · open dependents (with maturity label)
- **Relational Ghosting**: non-related cards drop to opacity 0.15; self/deps/dependents keep full opacity with tinted outlines (red/blue/gold)
- `[` / `]` keys step through highlighted set, updating iid in URL
- Edge-state banners for: filtered-out, disappeared after SSE, 404 on direct load

### Out of scope (non-goals)
- Editing issue fields from the UI (use `bd update` via copied command)
- New data source or live per-issue Dolt queries
- Board filter/sort/card-layout rework beyond the click interaction migration
- Issue authoring flows
- Dependency visualizations beyond the drawer and board ghosting

## Done definition

- Clicking a board card navigates to `/p/:pid/i/:iid`, opens the drawer, keeps board mounted
- Short-ID button copies id without opening detail; `c` shortcut same
- Desktop = drawer; narrow viewport = full-page
- Esc, outside click, X, and browser back all return to `/p/:pid` with prior search params preserved
- `GET /api/issue/:id?projectId=` returns detail from the snapshot file with no extra Dolt reads
- `_meta.schema_version` bumped; `snapshot-changed` payload carries `changedIssueIds[]`; selective invalidation verified
- Detail renders sanitized markdown; design field collapsed by default
- `Copy bd update command` action present and correct
- Dependency Weather rails present and populated correctly
- Board applies Relational Ghosting when drawer open; `[` / `]` cycles highlighted set
- Banners fire for filtered-out, disappeared, 404
- **Vitest** unit tests cover: API slice, SSE invalidation logic, short-ID button isolation, markdown sanitization, Dependency Weather rails, ghosting set calc
- **Playwright** e2e tests cover: click-to-open, close affordances, back-button preservation of search params, direct-load, `c` and `[` `]` shortcuts
- Manual pass confirms desktop drawer + narrow-screen full-page

## Edge cases

- Selected iid hidden by current board filters
- Selected iid deleted/disappeared after `snapshot-changed`
- Direct navigation to unknown iid
- `changedIssueIds[]` does not intersect open iid → drawer must not refetch
- Empty/missing description, notes, design, dependencies, or dependents
- Notes present only on closed dependencies (must render inline in that rail)
- Keyboard shortcuts must not conflict with text selection or focused inputs
- Copy-ID click and open-detail click must not both fire

## Implementation phases (ordered)

1. **Data contract + transport** — Zod schema + `_meta.schema_version` bump (`src/shared/snapshot-schema.ts`); `GET /api/issue/:id?projectId=` sliced from snapshot file (`src/server/app.ts`); `changedIssueIds[]` in SSE (`src/server/index.ts`)
2. **Route + shell** — `/p/$pid/i/$iid` child route (`web/src/router.tsx`); Radix Dialog drawer (desktop) / full-page (narrow); close affordances with search-param preservation
3. **Card interaction migration** — card click → open detail (`web/src/components/Card.tsx`); short-ID as own button; `c` shortcut
4. **Detail content** — read-only rendering; `react-markdown` + `remark-gfm` + `rehype-sanitize`; collapsed `design`; `Copy bd update command`
5. **Relationship context** — Dependency Weather block; Relational Ghosting on Board (`highlightIds` prop, CSS rule); `[`/`]` traversal
6. **Edge states + verification** — filtered-out/disappeared/404 banners; Vitest + Playwright suites; manual desktop/narrow pass

## Open questions for planning

None. The brainstorm resolved product, data, routing, rendering, and interaction decisions. Ready to plan.
acceptance_criteria:
- Given I'm on /p/alpha, when I click an issue card on desktop, then the URL becomes /p/alpha/i/<id>, board stays mounted, right drawer opens with issue detail
- Given a card is visible, when I click the short-ID button, then the ID is copied and the drawer does NOT open
- Given the drawer is open with ?priority=P2&q=foo in the URL, when I press Esc / click outside / click X / press browser back, then I return to /p/alpha?priority=P2&q=foo
- Given I open /p/alpha/i/<id> on a narrow viewport, then I see a full-page detail (no drawer)
- Given description/notes contain markdown, when the detail renders, then GFM is supported and unsafe HTML is sanitized
- Given an issue has a design field, when detail renders, then design is present but collapsed inside <details>
- Given an issue has dependencies/dependents, when I open detail, then I see three rails (open blockers, closed deps with inline notes, open dependents)
- Given the drawer is open, when the board renders behind it, then unrelated cards are opacity 0.15 and self/deps/dependents have tinted outlines
- Given the drawer is open with highlights, when I press [ or ], then focus/selection moves through the highlighted set and the URL iid updates
- Given the selected issue is filtered out by q/priority, when the detail is open, then a "hidden by filters" banner is shown with a clear-filter action
- Given the selected issue disappears after an SSE changedIssueIds tick, then a "no longer present" banner is shown
- Given I load /p/alpha/i/does-not-exist, then a 404 state renders inside the drawer while the board still renders
notes:
Phase 1 complete (2026-04-14): data contract + transport (description/notes/design/dependency_ids/dependent_ids in SnapshotIssueSchema v2; GET /api/issue/:id?projectId= slices snapshot file; SSE payload carries changedIssueIds[]). 432 tests green, mutation: derive 86.96%, snapshot-diff 90.91%.

Phase 2 complete (2026-04-14): /p/$pid/i/$iid TanStack child route + non-modal Radix Dialog drawer over mounted board. fetchIssue + IssueError taxonomy. Esc/X/outside-click/back close → preserve search params via search:(prev)=>prev navigation.

Phase 3 complete (2026-04-14): card-click navigates instead of copy. Short-id is now an inner <button> with stopPropagation (role=button, aria-label='Copy id <bd-id>'). 'c' key while card focused copies. Card threaded with projectId via Column→Board.

Tests: 448 vitest green, lint clean, 11/11 Playwright e2e green (drawer open/close/search-param preservation/direct-load/404/c-shortcut/short-id isolation).

Phases 4-6 remain: detail content (markdown render + design collapsed + Copy bd update), relationship context (Dependency Weather + Relational Ghosting + [/] traversal), edge states (filtered-out/disappeared banners + final mutation pass).

title: Add test suite with full coverage and mutation testing for helix-snapshot
description:
Problem: helix-snapshot (beads-helix-5ut) shipped with manual AC validation but no automated test suite. The 9 source files in src/ have zero test coverage. Without regression tests, future changes risk breaking the board-column derivation, atomic writes, or Dolt query logic silently.

DNA strand: convention-over-infrastructure — tests encode the conventions (derivation rules, error contract, file permissions) as executable specs.

Approach:
- Use Vitest as the test framework (fast, TypeScript-native, ESM support)
- Unit tests for each module: derive.ts, repo.ts, atomic.ts, dolt.ts, snapshot.ts, watch.ts, cli.ts
- Integration test: end-to-end refresh against live Dolt data
- Cover all 5 acceptance criteria (AC-01 through AC-05) as test cases
- Cover all 6 error contract scenarios from spec Section 8
- Cover edge cases from adversarial review: unknown status, missing project_id, path traversal, EXDEV fallback, stale lock, concurrent refresh
- Mutation testing via Stryker Mutator to verify test quality (target: mutation score >80%)
- Coverage target: line coverage >90%, branch coverage >85%

Done definition:
- npm test runs full suite and passes
- npm run test:coverage reports >90% line, >85% branch coverage
- npm run test:mutate runs Stryker and reports mutation score >80%
- All 5 ACs are encoded as named test cases
- All 6 error contract scenarios have dedicated tests
- CI-ready: tests run without manual setup beyond npm install + dolt available
notes: Depends on beads-helix-5ut (closed). Key test targets: src/derive.ts (board-column derivation — 7 status/label combos), src/atomic.ts (temp-rename, EXDEV fallback, O_EXCL lock, stale lock), src/dolt.ts (server-first transaction, direct fallback, JSON parse error), src/repo.ts (project_id validation, path traversal rejection). title: Implement helix-snapshot — lazy-refresh data backbone for dashboard renderers
description:
Build the helix-snapshot collector: a lazy-refresh component that reads beads issue state from Dolt, enriches it with board-column derivations and summary lines, and writes an atomic JSON snapshot to /tmp/beads-sidecar/<project_id>.snapshot.json.

Problem: Dashboard renderers (triage view, kanban board, tmux status, MCP resource) each need enriched issue data. Computing it per-render means repeated Dolt queries, duplicated board-column logic, and inconsistent results across views.

DNA strand: dual-format visibility — every feature serves both Claude and the developer. Also: convention over infrastructure — hardcoded conventions, nothing user-configurable in v1.

Approach (from multi-AI research + define):
- Two CLI commands: helix snapshot path and helix snapshot refresh
- Read via 2 SQL queries in a read-only transaction (server-first, direct dolt sql --disable-auto-gc fallback)
- Board column derivation: status wins (closed→done, in_progress→in_progress, deferred→deferred), else maturity label (ready/refined/idea, default idea)
- Invalidation: fs.watch on .beads/last-touched, 500ms debounce, 60s safety-net poll
- Atomic writes via write-to-temp-then-rename
- Node.js/TypeScript implementation
- No daemon, no network API, no write-back

Done definition:
- helix snapshot path resolves and prints the snapshot file path
- helix snapshot refresh generates a correct JSON snapshot with pre-computed board_column, columns_summary, summary_line
- Snapshot survives collector crash (atomic writes)
- Dolt failure preserves last good snapshot
- AC-01 through AC-05 in helix-snapshot-spec.md pass

Full spec: docs/specs/helix-snapshot-spec.md
notes: Promoted to ready: dual-format-visibility + convention-over-infrastructure. All 5 ACs pass. Multi-AI reviewed (Codex+Gemini+Sonnet). 4 review issues fixed.

title: Research: bd memories capabilities and cross-repo plugin potential
description: Explore what bd remember / bd memories unlocks beyond basic discovery persistence. Two angles: (1) What else can institutional memory do for us in this repo — e.g., can memories be queried programmatically, tagged, exported, or used in automation? (2) When beads-helix is installed as a plugin in other repos, how should institutional memory behave — scoped per-project, shared across projects, or layered? Investigate bd memories internals (storage format, search algorithm, capacity limits) and identify opportunities for helix to add convention or tooling on top.
notes:
## Research Findings (2026-04-11)

### Storage: Dolt config table, kv.memory.* namespace, VARCHAR(255) key + TEXT value
### Search: case-insensitive substring across key+value, no regex/fuzzy/tags
### Commands: remember [--key], memories [search] [--json], recall <key>, forget <key>
### Hidden: bd kv set 'memory.<key>' for bulk ops, bd recall for untruncated content
### Scoping: strictly per-project (.beads/embeddeddolt/hq/), no global store
### Cross-repo: repos.additional (experimental), federation, dolt push — none for memories specifically
### Recommendation: key naming convention (pattern:/decision:/gate:/lesson:/helix:) for category simulation, behavioral protocols for close rituals and promotion gate searches, plugin ships defaults in SKILL.md while projects accumulate in KV store
close_reason: Research complete. Key findings persisted as 4 bd memories (memories-internals-storage-and-api, memories-cross-repo-scoping-strategy, memories-key-naming-convention, memories-hidden-power-tools). Report saved to ticket notes.

title: Build refined issue template

title: Design: promotion triggers between maturity stages
description:
## Problem

Issues in beads-helix flow through three maturity labels (idea → refined → ready), but nothing prevents skipping stages or closing immature work. Developers forget to promote labels because no prompt exists at natural decision points.

## Solution

Four Claude Code hook-based triggers that surface promotion opportunities without blocking the developer's flow:

| Transition | Mechanism | Trigger Point |
|---|---|---|
| idea → refined | Manual | Developer runs `bd update` |
| refined → ready | Advisory | On `bd show` (1x/session/issue) |
| Close w/o `ready` | Advisory | On `bd close` |
| Close while `idea` | **Enforced** | On `bd close` (blocks, `--force` to override) |

## Design Principles

1. Natural touchpoints > scheduled checks — suggest at decision moments, not observation moments
2. One hard gate > three soft ones — only block close-while-idea
3. AI surfaces data, not decisions — "no acceptance criteria" vs "promoting to refined"
4. Compiler warnings, not errors — one-line stderr hints, never block the terminal
5. Escape hatches mandatory — `--force` bypasses gate, `--quiet` suppresses advisories

## Implementation

All behavior via Claude Code hooks. No beads CLI changes (layer, not fork). See docs/specs/promotion-triggers-spec.md for full FR/NFR spec.
design:
## Functional Requirements (8)

**FR-01** Manual idea→refined — no hooks, no gates, developer decides
**FR-02** Advisory on `bd show` for refined→ready — checks acceptance criteria + no open questions, 1x/session/issue
**FR-03** Advisory on `bd close` for close-without-ready — prints label, suggests promotion, non-blocking
**FR-04** Enforced gate on `bd close` for close-while-idea — blocks, requires --force
**FR-05** --force escape hatch — bypasses FR-04, logs friction event via bd remember
**FR-06** Session tracking — temp file or env var scoped to Claude Code session
**FR-07** --quiet suppression — suppresses FR-02/FR-03, not FR-04. Also via HELIX_QUIET=1
**FR-08** Suggestion logging — all advisories logged to bd remember with promotion-suggestion: prefix

## Non-Functional Requirements (4)

**NFR-01** Hook latency ≤500ms per invocation
**NFR-02** All output to stderr, single-line, prefixed [helix]
**NFR-03** No interference with existing bd stdout
**NFR-04** No beads CLI changes (hooks only)

## Edge Cases (must-handle)

- No maturity label → treat as pre-idea, close gate blocks
- Multiple maturity labels → use highest (ready > refined > idea), log warning
- Typo labels → treat as unlabeled (falls into no-label case)
- Session ID unavailable (outside Claude Code) → skip advisories silently
- bd show --json fails → hook exits 0, original command proceeds
- No .beads/ directory → hook short-circuits immediately
- Batch close (bd close id1 id2) → check each independently
- Re-closing already-closed issue → skip all maturity checks

## Out of Scope

- Auto-promotion (no automatic label changes)
- bd list integration (observation moment, not decision moment)
- Custom stage names (pipeline fixed at idea/refined/ready)
- Retrospective analytics on bd remember logs
acceptance_criteria:
## AC-1: Advisory on bd show (refined → ready)

Given an issue labeled `refined` with acceptance criteria and no "open question" in notes,
When user runs `bd show <id>`,
Then stderr prints: `[helix] <id> looks ready for promotion (has acceptance criteria, no open questions)`

Given an issue labeled `refined` without acceptance criteria,
When user runs `bd show <id>`,
Then no hint is printed.

## AC-2: Advisory on bd close (close without ready)

Given an issue labeled `refined`,
When user runs `bd close <id>`,
Then stderr shows current label and suggests promotion first, and close proceeds.

Given an issue labeled `ready`,
When user runs `bd close <id>`,
Then no advisory, close proceeds normally.

## AC-3: Enforced gate (close while idea)

Given an issue labeled `idea`,
When user runs `bd close <id>`,
Then command exits non-zero: `error: cannot close <id> at stage 'idea' — promote or use --force`

Given an issue labeled `idea`,
When user runs `bd close <id> --force`,
Then close proceeds and bd remember logs: `maturity-friction: closed idea-stage issue <id> with --force`

## AC-4: Escape hatches

Given any advisory would fire + --quiet passed,
Then advisory output (AC-1, AC-2) is suppressed. Enforced gate (AC-3) still blocks.

Given enforced gate would block + --force passed,
Then gate is bypassed.

## AC-5: Fail-safe

Given hook throws unexpected error,
When any bd command runs,
Then original command completes normally, error logged as single stderr warning.

## AC-6: Session deduplication

Given AC-1 hint already shown for <id> this session,
When user runs `bd show <id>` again,
Then hint is not repeated.
notes:
## Discovery Research Findings (2026-04-11)

### Recommended Design: Hybrid Triggers

| Transition | Mechanism | Trigger Point | Implementation |
|---|---|---|---|
| idea → refined | Manual | Developer decides | Existing `bd update` command |
| refined → ready | Advisory | `bd show` (1x/session/issue) | Claude Code hook |
| Close w/o `ready` | Advisory | `bd close` | Claude Code hook |
| Close while `idea` | **Enforced** | `bd close` | Claude Code hook + `--force` escape |

Friction score: 2/5

### Core Design Principles

1. **Natural touchpoints beat scheduled checks** — suggest at decision moments (`bd show`, `bd close`), not observation moments (`bd list`)
2. **One hard gate > three soft ones** — only block close-while-idea (skipped entire pipeline). Everything else advisory.
3. **AI surfaces data, not decisions** — "this issue has no acceptance criteria" (data) vs "promoting to refined" (decision)
4. **Compiler warnings, not errors** — one-line hints after command completes, never block the terminal
5. **Escape hatches mandatory** — `--force` bypasses hard gate, `--quiet` suppresses advisories

### Transition Details

- **idea→refined**: Fully manual, zero ceremony. Developer decides when fog clears.
- **refined→ready**: Advisory on `bd show`. Claude checks for acceptance criteria + no open questions. 1x/session/issue cap.
- **Close w/o ready**: Advisory on `bd close`. Print current label, ask if promotion should happen first. Non-blocking.
- **Close while idea**: Only hard gate. Require promotion or `--force`. Catches real mistake (skipped entire pipeline).

### Implementation Path

- All triggers via Claude Code hooks, not beads changes (layer, not fork)
- Advisory layer: hooks on `bd show` and `bd close` checking labels via `bd show --json`
- Enforced gate: pre-command hook on `bd close` checking label
- Log suggestion outcomes to `bd remember` for learning mode

### Evidence Base

- Linear/Shortcut: light git-event auto-promotion works for solo/small teams
- Jira: heavy gates kill solo-dev adoption
- CodeRabbit/Sourcery: severity-tiered actions (block/warn/suggest) proven pattern
- CodeRabbit recommends COMMENT mode before REQUEST_CHANGES
- Solo devs prefer AI as accelerator not autopilot (Osmani 2026)
- bd already supports labels, JSON output, label filtering — no beads-side work needed

### Open Questions

- Suggestion fatigue threshold (1x/session is inferred, not validated)
- Should `--force` close log a friction event automatically?
- Does the advisory on `bd show` feel natural or intrusive in practice?

title: Research: Claude Code plugin skeleton structure
description: Investigate the actual Claude Code plugin structure. What files are needed, what hooks are available, how skills/commands/agents are defined. Map the README's proposed architecture against what Claude Code actually supports.
notes: Research completed 2026-04-11. Key findings: Claude Code has 3-tier extensibility (Skills → Plugins → Marketplaces). Skills follow Agent Skills open standard (agentskills.io). Plugin bundle structure: .claude-plugin/plugin.json manifest + skills/ + hooks/ + agents/ + bin/. Hooks use stdin/stdout JSON protocol. Description field is the primary trigger mechanism. title: Replay mode — scrub project history as (PrimeBundle, diff, CloseCheckpoint) triples
description:

## Why this matters
- Git shows what changed. Commits don't show *why someone thought it should*.

- Onboarding + post-mortems + architectural retrospectives all become trivially queryable.

## Scope
- Record triples automatically via hooks into the session ledger
- UI: a timeline view with expand-on-hover into each triple
- Search by primed intent ('show me every time someone touched auth in the last 6 months')

## DNA strand
Dual-format visibility — humans scrub the timeline; Claude injects relevant past triples into new PrimeBundles.

- Archaeology mode (shares the retrieval substrate)

## Mechanism
- Hybrid retrieval: lexical over notes + vector over CloseCheckpoint summaries + graph expansion (deps, dependents, discovered-from).
- Index unit = synthesized document per closed issue (title, description, CloseCheckpoint, top memories, 1-hop graph context).

Dual-format visibility — Claude queries via MCP, developer queries via ⌘K palette.

- Session ledger

title: Fog-of-war — predictive collision routing for parallel agents
description:
Real-time visualization of which code regions have active agent modifications, plus interception when a new ticket claim would collide.

- Flight deck renders 'weather' per module: green (cold) / yellow (recent activity) / red (active modification).

title: Implement helix flight deck Level 2 — single-project read-only kanban
description:
Build the Level 2 UI for the helix flight deck: the single-project read-only kanban for mid-day pivoting. 3 columns (idea | refined | ready), cards sized for at-a-glance scanning.

Depends on: beads-helix-47u (shared infra — server, bridge, registry, SSE).

## In scope
- Route: /p/[projectId] rendered by TanStack Router
- Reads /api/snapshot?projectId=X on load
- Three-column CSS Grid layout: grid-template-columns: repeat(3, 1fr)
- Cards show: title (2-line clamp), priority chip (P0-P3 color-coded), short ID (mono), dep hint (e.g. '2↓ 1↑')
- Column header: stage name + count pill
- Read-only: no drag-drop, no inline edit, no CRUD endpoints
- Click card → copies bd ID to clipboard + toast confirmation (stretch: deep-links to a bead)
- Filter toolbar (client-side): priority filter, title search
- URL-reflects-state: ?priority=P0&q=foo via useSearchParams-equivalent from TanStack
- Manual refresh: 'R' keybind calls queryClient.invalidate or equivalent
- SSE live updates: subscribe to /api/events, invalidate on snapshot-changed for this projectId
- Priming header above the board: two-line density pattern from gh dash — project name + stage counts on line 1; 'last touched: <ticket>' + in_progress claim on line 2

## Card anatomy (from research — locked)
- Title (font-size: 0.9rem, 2-line clamp)
- Priority chip (colored, 'P0' / 'P1' / 'P2' / 'P3')
- Short ID (last segment of beads-helix-abc, monospace)
- Dep hint: '2↓ 1↑' (blockers / blocked-by) as single token
- NO assignee, NO last-updated, NO icon vocabulary, NO labels beyond stage

## Column sort default (locked)
priority DESC, updated_at DESC

## UX references (from research)
- Linear row density — 24px row height, mono ID leading, subtle 1px borders (oklch neutrals)
- Vercel dashboard card density — generous interior padding, tight exterior gutters, uppercase-tracked section labels
- Lazygit list→detail — one CSS grid, no modal stack
- GitHub Projects v2 — URL reflects selection, side drawer for detail (stretch)

## Component stack (same as Level 1)
- Tailwind v4 + hand-written cards + Radix primitives where needed + Sonner + Geist + Lucide
- @tanstack/react-router for routing + URL sync

## Empty states
- No snapshot yet: 'Run bd prime to generate snapshot' with a copy-command button
- Empty column: dim label 'no <stage> issues'
- Loading: skeleton rows matching card shape (not spinner)

## Explicitly out of scope
- Drag-and-drop between columns (Claude is the mutation surface)
- Inline editing of any field
- Staleness warnings on cards (cut per decisions 2026-04-14)
- Swimlanes, custom columns, filter presets
- Multi-project views (that's Level 1's job)

## Done definition
- `helix view` from inside a .beads/ project opens /p/<id> with the kanban visible
- All 3 columns render with correctly-bucketed cards
- Priority filter + search work via URL params
- Refresh (R key) works
- SSE live updates: update a ticket in another terminal, card reflects within 200ms
- Priming header shows correct stage counts + last-touched
- Empty states render correctly
- Keyboard navigation: tab through cards, enter to copy ID
- Component vitest tests for Card, Column, Board, FilterToolbar
- Playwright e2e covers: launch → board renders → filter → refresh → close

title: Implement /helix:close — close checkpoint with knowledge push
description:
Build the second slash command: /helix:close. This command verifies maturity stage at close time and systematically pushes findings to dependent tickets.

Problem: Knowledge flow between tickets only happens within a single session. When a ticket closes, its findings don't reach dependent tickets unless someone manually copies them. The current close hook blocks idea-stage closes but can't handle judgment calls (invalidated issues, exploratory work) — users bypass with --force.

DNA strand: convention-over-infrastructure — encodes the push-at-close knowledge flow pattern as a command.

Approach (from kfq research):
1. Build as a slash command that evaluates and reports (checkpoint, not wizard)
2. Verify maturity label before close — advisory, not blocking
3. Systematically push key findings to dependent tickets via bd update --notes
4. When this ships, demote the existing close hook from blocking to advisory

Done definition:
- /helix:close <id> evaluates maturity stage and knowledge flow obligations
- Prints scorecard of what's missing (dependent tickets without context, maturity gaps)
- Suggests bd close + bd update commands but never executes them
- Existing close hook demoted from blocking (exit 2) to advisory (warning only)

Sequenced after /helix:refine — build only when knowledge-flow friction repeats. See docs/research/slash-command-design.md for full research.
notes:
## Round-2 debate findings (2026-04-15)

Effort estimate revised from 'weekend' to ~3 days. Sandboxing + per-dependent iteration + dual-format output is non-trivial.

Must emit CloseCheckpoint JSON (see beads-helix-klt) alongside markdown. Schema: { schema_version, changed, learned, next, routed_to[] }. This prevents drift between Claude's view and the flight deck's view of what a close produced.

Must print 'bd update --notes' commands per open dependent, never execute them (Claude Code plugin sandboxing). Follow refine.md's pattern exactly.

Retrieval depends on push quality. This ticket is upstream of /helix:start (beads-helix-kse) — sloppy pushes make deterministic pulls worthless.

## Research Complete (2026-04-11)

Multi-AI research (Codex, Gemini, Claude) produced clear sequencing:

1. Build /helix:refine FIRST — checklist scorer, purest checkpoint-not-wizard, addresses ambiguous idea→refined transition
2. Build /helix:close SECOND — replaces problematic close hook with judgment-respecting command + knowledge push to dependents
3. DEFER /helix:triage — bd ready covers 80%, no friction point drives it yet
4. When /helix:close ships, demote close hook from blocking → advisory

Key framework: hard invariant → hook, judgment checkpoint → command, varying norm → convention. The close gate is currently a hook enforcing a judgment call (wrong mechanism → --force abuse).

Full research: docs/research/slash-command-design.md

title: Branch-per-Exploration — Dolt-native counterfactual trees for speculative work
description:
Speculative tickets get their own Dolt branch. Claude explores 3 approaches in parallel across 3 branches; the board renders a merge preview showing which world survives.

## Why this matters
- Issue tracking today is linear. Real design work is counterfactual.
- Dolt is already branchable — helix just needs to expose the mechanism.
- Claude (brainstorm 2026-04-20): 'issue tracking becomes counterfactual, not linear'.

## Scope (for idea stage, refinement will narrow)
- Command: helix explore <issue-id> --approach='A' / 'B' / 'C'
- Each approach = separate Dolt branch + separate worktree
- Deck shows a 'multiverse view' of the same ticket across branches
- Merge preview ranks branches by checkpoint scores

## DNA strand
Layer not fork — Dolt's branch primitive is the engine; helix ships the workflow opinion.

## Open questions
- Branch naming convention?
- How to CloseCheckpoint an abandoned branch?
- Does this collide with human git workflow, or sit alongside it?

title: Implement helix flight deck shared infrastructure — server, bridge, registry
description:
Build the load-bearing infrastructure shared by the Level 1 (cross-project) and Level 2 (single-project) flight deck views. Neither UI ticket can start until this lands.

Architecture: Vite SPA + tiny Node HTTP server (Prisma Studio / Drizzle Studio pattern — see docs/research/flight-deck-nextjs-2026-04-14.md for the Next.js vs SPA fork and rationale).

## In scope

1. **Hono server** serving the built SPA + JSON API. Routes:
   - `GET /` → SPA shell
   - `GET /api/snapshot?projectId=X` → reads /tmp/beads-sidecar/<id>.snapshot.json
   - `GET /api/registry` → reads ~/.helix/projects.json
   - `POST /api/registry` → atomic writes (upsert/remove), used by undo toast
   - `GET /api/events` → SSE stream of file-change events from fs.watch('/tmp/beads-sidecar/')
   - `GET /_helix-id` → self-identification (for debugging port collisions)
   - `POST /_shutdown` → token-validated shutdown target for navigator.sendBeacon

2. **CLI bridge** (src/commands/view.ts + src/bridge/*):
   - Context detection: findBeadsRepo(cwd) → level1 vs level2 routing hint
   - Ensure snapshot fresh (call existing refresh() from snapshot.ts)
   - Spawn Hono server as sub-process with env vars (HELIX_MODE, HELIX_PROJECT_ID, HELIX_REGISTRY_PATH, HELIX_SHUTDOWN_TOKEN, PORT)
   - Stdout ready-sentinel ('HELIX_READY port=N') for boot detection
   - open(url) via sindresorhus/open
   - SIGINT → child.kill('SIGTERM') → 3s grace → SIGKILL
   - Tab close → sendBeacon('/_shutdown', token) → server exits cleanly

3. **Registry** (src/bridge/registry.ts + src/shared/registry-schema.ts):
   - ~/.helix/projects.json schema v1 (see research doc for field list)
   - Atomic rename via proper-lockfile + write-to-tmp + fs.rename
   - Canonicalize paths via fs.realpathSync at registration
   - Lazy healing: status = active|missing|moved (never auto-purge)
   - CLI owns writes; UI reads + sends intent via POST /api/registry

4. **SSE live-update backbone**:
   - Server watches /tmp/beads-sidecar/ via fs.watch (debounced 200ms)
   - Emits 'snapshot-changed' events to connected clients
   - Client EventSource with reconnect on disconnect
   - No WebSockets — unidirectional data

5. **Port selection**: try 7373 → fallback to port:0 (OS-assigned). Log resolved URL.

6. **Undo toast plumbing (server side)**:
   - Registration write happens at CLI launch (before server starts)
   - Server exposes POST /api/registry with {action:'undo',id} to revert
   - Toast UX itself is the UI level's concern — just wire the endpoint

7. **Shared types** (src/shared/*):
   - snapshot-schema.ts (zod, re-exports from types.ts)
   - registry-schema.ts
   - paths.ts (~/.helix, /tmp/beads-sidecar)
   - Imported by both CLI and UI via tsconfig paths

8. **Packaging decision: bundle dist/ui/ into main npm tarball** (~5-10MB unpacked, 2-4MB compressed). Matches Prisma CLI and Drizzle Kit precedent. Can split to @helix/ui later if tarball grows >50MB.

## Explicitly out of scope (in downstream tickets)
- Level 1 UI — cross-project list with 'where I was / what's next' per project
- Level 2 UI — single-project read-only kanban (idea|refined|ready)
- Component library inventory (Tailwind v4, shadcn-copy, Radix, Sonner, Geist, Lucide) — handled in UI tickets

## Decisions locked (2026-04-14)
- Architecture: Vite SPA + Hono (not Next.js)
- Router: TanStack Router (UI tickets' concern)
- Split: three tickets (this one + 2 downstream)
- Live updates: YES in v1 (SSE — this ticket owns the backbone)
- Staleness card warnings: CUT
- Column sort default: priority DESC, updated_at DESC (applies in L2)
- Bundle strategy: single tarball (no @helix/ui split)

## Done definition
- `helix view` from inside a .beads/ project boots the server, auto-registers the project, opens the browser to localhost:7373/p/<id>
- `helix deck` (or `helix view` from a non-beads dir) opens localhost:7373/
- Ctrl+C cleanly shuts down the server (≤3s)
- Closing the tab shuts down the server via sendBeacon
- Concurrent helix view invocations from different shells don't corrupt ~/.helix/projects.json
- SSE clients receive 'snapshot-changed' within ~200ms of a snapshot file update
- All shared types pass through zod at server boundaries
- dist/ui/ is included in the published tarball
- Empty SPA HTML (placeholder) is served at / — real UIs land in downstream tickets
- Unit tests (Vitest) for port, registry, dispatcher; integration test with stub SPA

## Research artifacts
- docs/research/flight-deck-implementation-2026-04-14.md — original implementation research (server/kanban/registry/toast/prior art)
- docs/research/flight-deck-nextjs-2026-04-14.md — decision fork + SPA-vs-Next.js analysis
- docs/research/brainstorm-flight-deck-2026-04-14.md — original brainstorm + DNA rationale
notes:
## Session 2026-04-14: Phase 5-7 landed — ticket complete

Commits on main:
- ef5027e — Phase 5: CLI bridge (probe, launcher, shutdown, open-url, serve, view orchestrator, cli.ts wiring)
- 076f68d — Phase 6+7: packaging contract + integration tests + stub-SPA tests

Tests: 241 → 313 (+72 new). All green.

Done-definition — full verification:
- [x] helix view from .beads/ project → server boots, project auto-registers, browser opens /p/<id> (tests/cli-view.integration.test.ts)
- [x] helix deck / helix view from non-beads dir → opens /
- [x] Ctrl+C → graceful shutdown ≤3s (elapsed measured in integration test)
- [x] tab-close sendBeacon → /_shutdown token+Origin validated (negative path tested: wrong token = 403)
- [x] concurrent helix view from different shells → no registry corruption (100x fuzz + atomic rename from phase 1-2)
- [x] SSE clients get snapshot-changed within ~200ms (phase 3-4 integration test)
- [x] all shared types zod-validated at server boundaries
- [x] dist/ui/ in published tarball (tests/packaging.test.ts verifies via npm pack --json)
- [x] empty SPA placeholder at /
- [x] Unit tests for port (probe), registry, dispatcher (view orchestrator); integration test with stub SPA

Key design decisions this session:
1. **CLI spawns `helix serve` as subprocess** — the same binary handles both CLI and server roles. Launcher dispatches via `[cliPath, 'serve']`.
2. **Parent env NOT inherited** — launcher starts child with a minimal env (PATH/HOME/USER/NODE_ENV) + HELIX_* config. Prevents stray HELIX_* vars in the caller's shell from overriding explicit config.
3. **Port 7373 + fallback** — `HELIX_PORT=7373` by default; tests override with `HELIX_PREFERRED_PORT=0` to avoid collisions.
4. **openUrl refuses non-http(s)** — guards against command injection if a URL ever flows in from untrusted source.
5. **Orphan-child defense** — view.ts registers process 'exit' handler that kills the child on any clean parent exit. SIGKILL of parent remains undetectable (POSIX limitation), but uncaught exceptions no longer leave servers on 7373.
6. **Test-mode env escape hatches** — HELIX_SKIP_OPEN / HELIX_PREFERRED_PORT / HELIX_REGISTRY_PATH / HELIX_SIDECAR_DIR let integration tests isolate without touching the real ~/.helix or launching browsers.

Architecture summary for downstream UI tickets (beads-helix-vm2, beads-helix-wso):
- Server is ready at http://localhost:<port>/ — vite SPA build drops into dist/ui/ and is picked up automatically
- API contract: GET /api/snapshot?projectId=X, GET /api/registry, POST /api/registry {action:'upsert'|'remove'|'undo'}, GET /api/events (SSE), GET /_helix-id
- sendBeacon target: POST /_shutdown with raw token text body, Origin must match Host
- Routing: / → deck, /p/<id> → project kanban (client-side routing via TanStack Router)

Capture quality is upstream of surfacing quality — a deterministic /helix:start that pulls sloppy CloseCheckpoint notes just propagates noise faster. 'Garbage routing is worse than missing routing.' helix view spawns helix serve as subprocess via [cliPath, 'serve']. Parent env is NOT inherited beyond PATH/HOME/USER/NODE_ENV — launcher scrubs to prevent stray HELIX_* vars in caller's shell from overriding config. Test-mode env escape hatches: HELIX_SKIP_OPEN=1 (no-op openUrl), HELIX_PREFERRED_PORT=0 (avoid 7373 collisions), HELIX_REGISTRY_PATH, HELIX_SIDECAR_DIR.

bd memories are strictly per-project (.beads/ dir). No global memory layer exists. Cross-repo mechanisms: repos.additional (experimental, issues only), bd federation (peer-to-peer Dolt sync), bd dolt push (remote sync). For beads-helix plugin: ship universal knowledge in SKILL.md (versioned), store project-specific learning in KV with helix.* prefix, upstream generalizable discoveries via PR to the plugin repo. The PR is the memory promotion mechanism.

project-id: 717bd613-5bc3-4245-b857-db37b0bf6ef4 is the beads-helix project id. Use in dev URLs like http://localhost:5173/p/717bd613-5bc3-4245-b857-db37b0bf6ef4. Source of truth is .beads/metadata.json project_id field.

8ua phase 2 gotcha: TanStack Router Link in unit tests requires a RouterProvider. Created web/src/test-utils.tsx withRouter() helper wrapping with createMemoryHistory + a /p/$projectId + i/$issueId route tree. Also: synchronous getByText fails after wrapping in Router (router mounts async) — tests must use findByText/findByRole.

helix: /helix:close is a 3-day job, not a weekend. Claude Code plugin sandboxing means you can only PRINT 'bd update --notes' commands per dependent, never execute them (same pattern as refine.md). With parsing unstructured bd show output + per-dependent summary composition + dual-format JSON+markdown emission, minimum 8-10 tool calls per close. Budget accordingly.

knowledge-flow-forward: When working on a ticket, ALWAYS read its forward dependencies (tickets that depend on THIS one) before starting. Understanding what downstream tickets need as output shapes the work — it's not just about what this ticket asks for, but what the next ticket expects to receive. The pull-at-start pattern works in both directions: pull from closed deps (what was learned) AND pull from open dependents (what they need from us).

bd memories stores entries in the Dolt config table as kv.memory.<slug> (VARCHAR 255 key, TEXT value). Search is case-insensitive substring match across both key and value. No tags, timestamps, or structured queries. Key auto-generated from first ~7 words if --key omitted (collision risk). Commands: bd remember '<text>' [--key <slug>], bd memories [search] [--json], bd recall <key> (full untruncated content), bd forget <key>. Undocumented: bd kv set 'memory.<key>' '<val>' for bulk ops. bd prime injects all memories under '## Persistent Memories (N)'. No capacity limits. Per-project scoped to .beads/embeddeddolt/hq/, no global memory store.

Recommended key naming convention for structured memories: pattern:<topic> (team conventions), decision:<topic> (architectural choices), gate:<stage>:<topic> (promotion gates), lesson:<topic> (hard-won lessons), helix:<topic> (plugin-level insights). bd memories <prefix> returns all matching entries — grep-powered categorization with zero infrastructure. Always use explicit --key to avoid auto-slug collisions.

flight-deck architecture (beads-helix-47u): chose Vite SPA + tiny Node server over Next.js. Rationale: Prisma Studio and Drizzle Studio — the two closest 'local UI for local data' analogues — both chose SPA+server, not Next.js. Next.js-specific features (RSC, file-based routing) not load-bearing for 2 views over static JSON. Tarball 5-10MB vs 50MB; cold start 50-150ms vs 400-800ms; no SWC/sharp platform-binary trap. Component ergonomics (Tailwind+Radix+React) identical. Decision recorded in docs/research/flight-deck-nextjs-2026-04-14.md.

hooks-json-protocol: Hooks receive JSON on stdin ({tool_name, tool_input}), return JSON on stdout ({hookSpecificOutput: {permissionDecision, updatedInput, additionalContext}}). Exit codes: 0=success, 2=deny, 3=ask. Plugin hooks use ${CLAUDE_PLUGIN_ROOT} for path resolution.

sidecar path gotcha: src/repo.ts had hardcoded /tmp/beads-sidecar while src/shared/paths.ts uses os.tmpdir(). On macOS these diverge (/tmp vs /var/folders/.../T), causing snapshot writer/reader to miss each other. Unified on sidecarDir() from shared/paths.ts.

skill-description-is-trigger: The YAML frontmatter 'description' field is THE primary mechanism for Claude to decide when to invoke a skill proactively. Front-load capability info (truncated at ~250 chars in listings). Include explicit trigger conditions. This is not just metadata.

agent-skills-standard: Claude Code skills follow the Agent Skills open standard (agentskills.io), portable across Claude Code, Cursor, Gemini CLI, Codex CLI, and GitHub Copilot. Key frontmatter: name, description (required), allowed-tools, user-invocable, argument-hint, context: fork, disable-model-invocation (optional).

flight-deck-level-2-pattern: web/ sibling dir + vite (root=web) + tailwind v4 (@theme + @import) + tanstack-router code-based + tanstack-react-query + sonner. Test infra: vitest projects (node + jsdom), clipboard wrapper helper to bypass jsdom 25 getter quirk, MockEventSource for SSE, playwright with dist/ui/ as HELIX_UI_DIR. cli.ts auto-discovers dist/ui/ relative to import.meta.url so helix view ships the SPA without explicit env vars.

When n9j (research) closed and unblocked 221, the research findings only reached 221 because we happened to be in the same session. No mechanism pushed findings forward. Fixed with push-at-close + pull-at-start convention in CLAUDE.md. This pattern is a strong candidate for helix automation — a /helix:close or hook that prompts for summary push to blocked tickets.

plugin-distribution: Marketplaces are git repos with .claude-plugin/marketplace.json. Install: claude plugin install <name>@<marketplace> --scope user|project|local. Submit to official: claude.ai/settings/plugins/submit. Scopes: project (.claude/skills/ in repo), personal (~/.claude/skills/), marketplace (git).

promotion-suggestion: suggested promoting beads-helix-18b from refined to ready on bd show

SIGKILL of helix view parent CLI leaves the server subprocess orphaned on port 7373. view.ts registers process 'exit' + SIGTERM handlers that kill the child, covering uncaught-exception paths. True SIGKILL is undetectable in userland (POSIX limitation) — no defense possible. If you see a leftover helix on 7373, it's likely an orphaned test subprocess; kill by PID.

flight-deck-server (beads-helix-47u phase 3-4): proper-lockfile gotcha — within a single Node process, concurrent lock attempts on the same file fail with 'Lock file is already being held' because proper-lockfile tracks locks in process-local state. Fix: wrap calls in an in-process mutex keyed by file path (src/bridge/registry.ts withPathMutex). Handles both cross-process and same-process concurrency.

helix: canonical JSON schemas (PrimeBundle, CloseCheckpoint) must ship in v1 in src/shared/types/, not deferred to v2. Without them, Claude's markdown view and the flight-deck UI view drift and both stop being trusted. Drift bites first at memory provenance and dependency summaries. This is load-bearing, not polish. See beads-helix-klt and R2 debate synthesis.
maturity-friction: closed idea-stage issue beads-helix-18b with --force

maturity-friction: /typeset skill produces bounded, single-session UI diffs. Creating a bead retrospectively after the work felt like ceremony tax for a sub-session change. Worth considering whether the pipeline should have an escape hatch for <30min UI polish passes.

maturity-friction: bug tickets filed with full repro + AC + root cause often skip the idea/refined stages entirely. When a bug is found mid-flight with diagnosis attached, it's born 'ready' — forcing it through idea→refined is ceremony. Consider: bug type should bypass the maturity pipeline, or have a shorter path (idea → ready, skip refined when AC present).

bd labels fully support maturity pipeline (idea/refined/ready) with no beads-side work needed. Key commands: create with -l flag (bd create -l idea), filter with bd list -l <label> or --label-any for OR, update with bd update <id> --add-label refined --remove-label idea. Also: bd label list-all shows counts, bd label propagate cascades parent labels to children. AND/OR filtering, glob patterns (--label-pattern), and regex (--label-regex) all work.

claude-plugin-skeleton: Plugin structure is .claude-plugin/plugin.json (manifest) + skills/ (SKILL.md dirs) + hooks/hooks.json + agents/ + bin/ + .mcp.json + .lsp.json + settings.json. Plugin skills auto-namespace as /plugin-name:skill-name. Test with: claude --plugin-dir ./my-plugin. Hot-reload: /reload-plugins.

bd recall <key> is undocumented but critical — retrieves full untruncated memory content by exact key (bd memories truncates display). bd kv is the underlying store — bd kv list shows memories as memory.<key>, bd kv set 'memory.<key>' '<val>' creates memories directly (enables bulk import/automation without bd remember). bd memories --json returns {key: value} map with full values.

skill-vs-command: Two parallel systems. Commands = single .md in ~/.claude/commands/ (dir nesting → colon namespace). Skills = directories with SKILL.md + optional scripts/, references/, assets/ in ~/.claude/skills/. Skills support progressive disclosure; commands are flat files.

snapshot-schema-migration: bumping _meta.schema_version (2+) requires updating ALL web test fixtures (web/src/**/*.test.{ts,tsx}) that construct Snapshot objects inline — not just factory functions. Miss any and Board/ProjectPage tests fail with Zod 'Too small' errors. Pattern: grep for 'schema_version:' across web/src/ after any schema bump.

bd list --json gotchas: (1) dependencies field is ABSENT not empty-array when count=0, (2) assignee is ABSENT not null when unset, (3) metadata inside dependencies is a JSON string needing double-parse, (4) bd show --json returns a list too but omits count fields, (5) closed issues add close_reason/closed_at/acceptance_criteria/notes fields
